questions.q: Which set is the upper half-plane $\mathbb H$?
Why is the real axis special in the model?
choices: It is the ideal boundary at infinity
It is a geodesic inside $\mathbb H$
It is the set of elliptic fixed points
questions.explain: The boundary is not in the space but appears as ideal points.

questions.q: The line element in $\mathbb H$ is:
questions.explain: Hyperbolic length scales Euclidean length by 1/y.

questions.q: At height $y=2$, what is the scaling factor on Euclidean length?
As $y\to 0^+$, short Euclidean segments have hyperbolic length:
questions.explain: Division by $y$ makes lengths diverge near boundary.

questions.q: Which curves are geodesics in $\mathbb H$?
choices: Vertical lines and semicircles orthogonal to the real axis
questions.explain: This is the defining geometric picture of the model.
Every vertical line in H is geodesic.

questions.q: How many intersection points can two distinct geodesics in H have inside H?
questions.explain: Like Euclidean lines, distinct geodesics intersect at most once in the interior.
This is the Möbius (fractional linear) action.
This is the entry point to modular forms.

questions.q: The kernel of the $SL_2(\mathbb R)$ action on $\mathbb H$ is:
questions.explain: Both $g$ and $-g$ give the same Möbius map, so the effective group is $PSL_2(\mathbb R)=SL_2(\mathbb R)/\{\pm I\}$.

questions.q: An element $g\in PSL_2(\mathbb R)$ with $|\operatorname{tr} g|<2$ is:
choices: parabolic (one fixed point at the boundary)
elliptic (a single fixed point inside $\mathbb H$)
questions.explain: Trace-squared minus 4 is the discriminant of the fixed-point quadratic; negative discriminant puts the fixed point inside $\mathbb H$.
$\operatorname{tr} g = 1+1 = 2$, so $g$ is parabolic with its unique fixed point at $\infty$.
